1.event: Julius Caesar introduces the Julian calendar
Ellis Island opens as an immigration station in New York
Cuba's revolution succeeds as Batista flees and Castro takes power

2.event: Granada falls to Spain, ending Muslim rule in Iberia
Georgia becomes the 4th U.S. state
Isaac Asimov, science fiction author, is born

3.event: Martin Luther is excommunicated from the Catholic Church
Alaska is purchased by the United States from Russia
Alaska becomes the 49th U.S. state

4.event: Isaac Newton is born in England
Samuel Colt sells his first revolver to the U.S. government
The United States launches Intelsat 1, the first commercial communications satellite

5.event: Halley's Comet appears, later seen as an omen of the Norman conquest
American forces defeat the British at the Battle of Cowpens
Henry Ford introduces the $5 workday, doubling workers' wages

6.event: Harold II is crowned King of England
Samuel Morse and Alfred Vail publicly demonstrate the telegraph
The first Superman comic book is published

7.event: Aztecs found Tenochtitlán, modern-day Mexico City
Fahrenheit invents the mercury thermometer
First transatlantic telephone call is made between New York and London

8.event: Pope Innocent III begins his papacy

9.event: Romulus Augustulus becomes the last Roman Emperor of the West
Connecticut becomes the 5th U.S. state

10.event: Julius Caesar crosses the Rubicon river, starting a Roman civil war

12.event: Hernán Cortés lands in Mexico to begin his conquest
Jack London, author of The Call of the Wild, is born

14.event: Edward I's son becomes the first Prince of Wales

The first nuclear submarine USS Nautilus is commissioned

15.event: Elizabeth I is crowned Queen of England
Vermont declares independence from Britain and becomes a republic
Martin Luther King Jr. is born in Atlanta, Georgia

16.event: Octavian receives the title of Augustus, marking the start of the Roman Empire
Ivan the Terrible is crowned as the first Tsar of Russia

17.event: Pope Gregory XI returns the papacy to Rome from Avignon
James Cook becomes the first person to cross the Antarctic Circle

18.event: Captain Cook discovers the Hawaiian Islands

The first airmail flight in India takes place in Allahabad

19.event: Rouen surrenders to Henry V of England

20.event: The first English Parliament is convened by Simon de Montfort

Elizabeth Blackwell becomes the first woman to receive a medical degree in the U.S.

Gold is discovered at Sutter's Mill, triggering the California Gold Rush

25.event: Henry VIII secretly marries Anne Boleyn

The first Winter Olympics opens in Chamonix, France

26.event: Portuguese explorer Pedro Álvares Cabral discovers Brazil

27.event: Trajan becomes Roman Emperor, beginning Rome's golden age

28.event: Ferdinand Magellan is killed in the Philippines during his circumnavigation

3.event: Bartolomeu Dias rounds the Cape of Good Hope

12.event: Lady Jane Grey is executed after just nine days as Queen of England

23.event: Pliny the Elder, Roman naturalist and author, is born
The Ottoman army begins its siege of Vienna
Coca-Cola launches 'New Coke', replacing its original formula (to public outrage)

24.event: John Cabot departs from Bristol on his voyage to North America
Napoleon leads the Grande Armée across the Russian border
The Soviet Union begins the Berlin Blockade

25.event: Vikings raid Rouen in the Seine Valley
The Battle of Little Bighorn: Custer's Last Stand begins
North Korea invades South Korea, beginning the Korean War

26.event: The Jacquerie peasant revolt erupts in France
The United Nations Charter is signed in San Francisco by 50 nations
Elvis Presley gives his final concert in Indianapolis, Indiana

27.event: The Jacquerie peasant revolt spreads across France
George II of Great Britain is the last British king to lead troops into battle at Dettingen
Canadian researcher discovers the first hole in the ozone layer over the Arctic

28.event: Crusaders capture Antioch during the First Crusade
Peter III of Russia is overthrown by his wife Catherine the Great
Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria is assassinated in Sarajevo, triggering WWI

29.event: An annular solar eclipse occurs on the first day of summer
The Globe Theatre in London burns to the ground during a performance of Henry VIII
The Oklahoma City bombing federal building is demolished

30.event: Suleiman the Magnificent becomes Ottoman Sultan
The Tunguska event — a massive explosion in Siberia, Russia, flattens 2,000 square kilometers of forest
The first African American medalist at the Berlin Olympics: Jesse Owens wins gold

1.event: The Union of Lublin creates the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth
Canada becomes a self-governing dominion within the British Empire
Hong Kong is handed back to China by Britain

2.event: Li Shimin kills his brothers in the Xuanwu Gate Incident, taking power in Tang Dynasty China
The Continental Congress votes for American independence from Britain
The Civil Rights Act is signed into law by President Johnson, banning racial discrimination

3.event: Saladin captures Jerusalem's water supply at the Battle of Hattin
George Washington takes command of the Continental Army in Cambridge
The USS Vincennes shoots down Iran Air Flight 655, killing 290 civilians

4.event: A supernova observed by Chinese and Arab astronomers creates what becomes the Crab Nebula
The United States Declaration of Independence is adopted
John Adams and Thomas Jefferson both die on this day — the 50th anniversary of independence

5.event: Marco Polo returns to Venice after 24 years of travels in Asia
Isaac Newton publishes Principia Mathematica, establishing classical mechanics
Elvis Presley records his first commercial song, 'That's Alright Mama'

6.event: King Henry II of England dies, succeeded by Richard I (Lionheart)
Jan Hus, Czech religious reformer, is burned at the stake
The Piper Alpha oil platform explodes in the North Sea, killing 167

7.event: A retrial posthumously acquits Joan of Arc of heresy
The first English colony in the New World is established on Roanoke Island
Boris Becker becomes the youngest player and first unseeded player to win Wimbledon

8.event: Crusaders arrive in Jerusalem and begin their siege of the holy city
Sweden suffers a catastrophic defeat at the Battle of Poltava in Ukraine
Reports emerge of a crashed UFO near Roswell, New Mexico

9.event: Emperor Avitus is proclaimed the ruler of the Western Roman Empire
George Washington orders the Declaration of Independence to be read to American troops
South Sudan gains independence from Sudan, becoming the world's newest country

10.event: Roman Emperor Hadrian dies, succeeded by Antoninus Pius
Lady Jane Grey is deposed and Mary I takes the English throne
Telstar 1 is launched, enabling the first live trans-Atlantic TV broadcast

11.event: The explorer Zheng He sets sail on his first voyage from China
Napoleon Bonaparte captures Malta from the Knights of St. John
Skylab, America's first space station, falls from orbit over Australia

12.event: Richard I of England arrives in the Holy Land during the Third Crusade
Confederate raider John Hunt Morgan begins his Ohio raid
The Viking 1 spacecraft enters Martian orbit

13.event: Julius Caesar is born in Rome
Jean-Paul Marat, French revolutionary leader, is assassinated in his bathtub
Live Aid concerts in London and Philadelphia raise $40 million for famine relief in Africa

14.event: Louis VIII becomes King of France
The Bastille is stormed in Paris, beginning the French Revolution
The Apollo 11 crew splashes down safely in the Pacific Ocean

15.event: Crusaders capture Jerusalem in the First Crusade, massacring its Muslim and Jewish inhabitants
The Great Wall of China is formally opened as a tourist attraction
Saddam Hussein becomes President of Iraq

16.event: Muhammad's Hijra: the Prophet leaves Mecca for Medina, marking the start of the Islamic calendar
The Potsdam Conference between the Big Three Allied leaders begins
Apollo 11 launches from Kennedy Space Center, carrying astronauts to the Moon

17.event: Joan of Arc leads French victory at the Battle of Patay
Spain cedes Florida to the United States
Disneyland opens in Anaheim, California

18.event: Visigoths sack Rome for the first time in 800 years
The Spanish Civil War begins
The Beatles found Apple Corps Ltd. in London

19.event: Francesco Petrarca (Petrarch), the first humanist scholar, dies

20.event: Francesco Petrarch is born, considered the first modern poet
Colombia declares independence from Spain
Apollo 11 astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin become the first humans to walk on the Moon

21.event: A tsunami devastates Alexandria, Egypt following a massive earthquake
The first Battle of Bull Run, the first major land battle of the American Civil War
Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin land on the Moon's Sea of Tranquility

22.event: Godfrey of Bouillon becomes the first Christian ruler of Jerusalem
The Battle of the Somme begins, one of WWI's bloodiest battles
Tim Berners-Lee makes the World Wide Web available to the general public

23.event: William Burt patents the typographer, a precursor to the typewriter
Ulysses S. Grant, 18th U.S. President, dies of throat cancer
The Helsinki Olympic Games open in Finland

24.event: The city of Caracas, capital of Venezuela, is founded

Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin return safely to Earth after their Moon landing

25.event: Constantine I is proclaimed Emperor of Rome by his troops in York, England
Henry IV of France converts to Catholicism to take the throne
The first flight of the Boeing 747SP, the world's longest range commercial aircraft

26.event: The Battle of Siffin occurs between Ali ibn Abi Talib and Muawiyah I
The U.S. Post Office is established with Benjamin Franklin as Postmaster General
Lance Armstrong wins his 7th consecutive Tour de France

27.event: The Battle of Bouvines: Philip II of France defeats a coalition against him
The first successful transatlantic telegraph cable is completed
The Korean War armistice is signed, ending three years of fighting

28.event: The Great Schism splits Christianity into Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox
Maximilien Robespierre, architect of the Reign of Terror, is arrested and executed
The Summer Olympics open in Los Angeles, California

29.event: The Spanish Armada sets sail from Lisbon to invade England
The Melbourne City, Australia is officially established

30.event: Baghdad is founded by Caliph Al-Mansur as the capital of the Abbasid Caliphate

The final U.S. personnel evacuate from Saigon as Vietnam falls to communist forces

31.event: Charlemagne's son Louis is crowned King of Aquitaine
Akbar the Great becomes Mughal Emperor of India at age 13
The Soviet Union officially dissolves and the Commonwealth of Independent States is established

1.event: Cleopatra VII of Egypt commits suicide after the conquest by Octavian (Augustus)
Joseph Priestley discovers oxygen (though Scheele discovered it first)
MTV launches with the first music video 'Video Killed the Radio Star'

2.event: Battle of Cannae: Hannibal defeats Rome in one of history's worst military disasters
Members of the Continental Congress sign the Declaration of Independence
Iraq invades Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War

3.event: Christopher Columbus sets sail from Palos de la Frontera, Spain
The United States and Soviet Union sign the Limited Nuclear Test Ban Treaty
The Statue of Liberty reopens to the public after 9/11 security upgrades

4.event: The future King Edward I of England is born
The literary magazine 'Saturday Evening Post' publishes its first issue

5.event: The Battle of Maserfield: King Oswald of Northumbria is killed
Cyrus West Field completes the first transatlantic telegraph cable
Curiosity rover successfully lands on Mars

6.event: The Pied Piper of Hamelin legend is said to have occurred on this date
The United States drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, killing 80,000 people instantly
Tim Berners-Lee publishes the first website on the World Wide Web

7.event: Aristotle, one of the greatest philosophers of antiquity, dies
George Washington creates the Order of the Purple Heart, the oldest military award still given
U.S. embassies in Kenya and Tanzania are bombed by Al-Qaeda, killing 224

8.event: Tycho Brahe's observatory Uraniborg is completed in Denmark

9.event: Roman Emperor Valens is killed by the Visigoths at the Battle of Adrianople

10.event: Nineveh, capital of the Assyrian Empire, is sacked and destroyed
The Swedish warship Vasa sinks on her maiden voyage in Stockholm harbor
The Beatles release 'Revolver', considered one of the greatest albums of all time

11.event: The start of the Mesoamerican Long Count Calendar (Maya calendar)

The last total solar eclipse of the 20th century occurs over Europe

12.event: Cleopatra VII of Egypt dies, ending the Ptolemaic Dynasty

13.event: Hormizd IV becomes King of Persia

14.event: The Peace of Constance grants independence to the cities of northern Italy

15.event: Roland, a Frankish military commander, is killed at the Battle of Roncevaux Pass

Japan surrenders, ending World War II (V-J Day)

16.event: Louis IV, Holy Roman Emperor, takes possession of Rome

17.event: Catherine the Great becomes Empress of Russia
The first running of the Brighton & Hove Albion horse race

18.event: Virginia Dare becomes the first English child born in the Americas

25.event: The First Ecumenical Council of Nicaea concludes

26.event: English forces defeat France at the Battle of Crécy during the Hundred Years' War

The Camp David Accords between Egypt and Israel are announced

27.event: British forces defeat George Washington's army at the Battle of Long Island

Lord Mountbatten is assassinated by the IRA while fishing off the coast of Ireland

28.event: Romulus Augustulus becomes the last Emperor of Rome

31.event: Byzantine Empress Theodora dies, ending the Macedonian dynasty

2.event: Battle of Actium: Octavian defeats Mark Antony and Cleopatra

Japan formally surrenders, ending World War II

3.event: San Marino, one of the world's smallest nations, is founded

6.event: The Olympic Games are abolished by Theodosius I after 1,168 years

2.event: Saladin recaptures Jerusalem from the Crusaders

2.event: Tang Emperor Gaozu has his son Li Yuanji poisoned

Charles Darwin publishes 'On the Origin of Species'
Lee Harvey Oswald, accused assassin of JFK, is shot and killed by Jack Ruby on live TV

25.event: The Battle of Montgisard: Baldwin IV of Jerusalem defeats Saladin
Granada, the last Moorish kingdom in Spain, surrenders to Ferdinand and Isabella
Kenya gains independence from Britain

26.event: Charlemagne defeats the Saxons at the Battle of Detmold
Mary Wollstonecraft, mother of Mary Shelley, dies
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster's extent is finally acknowledged to the international community

27.event: Pope Urban II calls for the First Crusade at the Council of Clermont
New Amsterdam is officially renamed New York
Harvey Milk and Mayor George Moscone are assassinated in San Francisco

28.event: The Kalemegdan fortress falls to the Ottoman Turks
Ravel's Bolero premieres in Paris
Margaret Thatcher resigns as British Prime Minister after 11 years

29.event: Cardinal Wolsey dies on his way to face trial for treason in London
Richard Byrd and crew become the first to fly over the South Pole
The UN votes to partition Palestine into Jewish and Arab states

30.event: The Great Northern War begins when Russia, Denmark and Poland attack Sweden
The preliminary articles of peace ending the American Revolution are signed in Paris
The Algerian War of Independence begins with a series of attacks on French targets

1.event: Charlemagne, King of the Franks, is crowned Holy Roman Emperor by Pope Leo III
The Ford Motor Company introduces the first moving assembly line
Rosa Parks refuses to give up her seat on a bus in Montgomery, Alabama

2.event: Hernán Cortés, Spanish conqueror of the Aztec Empire, dies
Napoleon Bonaparte crowns himself Emperor of France in Notre Dame Cathedral
The world's first man-made nuclear reactor, Chicago Pile-1, achieves criticality

3.event: Pope John X crowns Berengar I as Emperor of Rome
Illinois becomes the 21st U.S. state
The Bhopal disaster: a gas leak at a pesticide plant in India kills over 3,000 people

4.event: Henry II becomes King of England, founding the Plantagenet dynasty
The Observer, the world's first Sunday newspaper, is published in Britain
The first U.S. commercial test for DNA fingerprinting is conducted

5.event: Roman Emperor Augustus is born (as Gaius Octavius)
A retrial rehabilitates Joan of Arc, posthumously acquitting her of heresy
Flight 19, a group of U.S. Navy bombers, disappears in the Bermuda Triangle

6.event: Mongols sack and burn Kiev, one of the great cultural centers of the era
The 13th Amendment abolishing slavery is ratified in the United States
President Mikhail Gorbachev resigns, formally dissolving the Soviet Union

7.event: Marcus Tullius Cicero, the great Roman orator, is assassinated
Japan attacks the U.S. naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, bringing the U.S. into WWII
Reagan and Gorbachev sign the INF Treaty, eliminating intermediate-range nuclear missiles

8.event: The Inca leader Atahualpa is executed by Francisco Pizarro in Peru
The American Federation of Labor (AFL) is founded
John Lennon, former Beatle and peace activist, is shot and killed in New York City

9.event: Sancho II of Portugal is deposed by his brother Alfonso III
Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen suffers a stroke that leads to his death
Prince Charles and Princess Diana announce their separation

10.event: Pope Leo X excommunicates Martin Luther from the Catholic Church
The first Nobel Prizes are awarded in Stockholm and Oslo
Nelson Mandela receives the Nobel Peace Prize in Oslo, Norway

11.event: Byzantine Emperor Nikephoros II Phokas is assassinated by his wife and her lover
King Edward VIII abdicates the British throne for love of Wallis Simpson
Apollo 17 launches — the last time humans would travel to the Moon

12.event: The Byzantine Empire decisively defeats Persia at the Battle of Nineveh
Guglielmo Marconi receives the first transatlantic radio transmission
The U.S. Supreme Court halts Florida's presidential vote recount, effectively giving Bush the presidency

13.event: King Louis IX of France is captured by Muslims during the Seventh Crusade
Francis Drake begins his circumnavigation of the globe
Saddam Hussein is captured by U.S. forces near Tikrit, Iraq

14.event: Constantinople is severely damaged by an earthquake
Roald Amundsen's team becomes the first to reach the South Pole
Live Aid raises over $100 million for African famine relief

15.event: Caligula is proclaimed Roman Emperor after the death of Tiberius
The U.S. Bill of Rights is ratified, guaranteeing fundamental freedoms
The Wizard of Oz premieres in Hollywood, California

16.event: Pepin of Herstal, Frankish mayor of the palace, dies
The Boston Tea Party: colonists dump British tea into Boston Harbor
The Battle of the Bulge begins — Germany's last major offensive on the Western Front

17.event: Timur (Tamerlane) sacks Delhi, killing 100,000 people in one day
The Wright Brothers achieve their first successful powered flight at Kitty Hawk
The Simpsons airs its first full episode on Fox

18.event: Macrinus is defeated by forces loyal to young Emperor Elagabalus
The 13th Amendment abolishing slavery is ratified by Georgia, completing ratification
The hit film Jaws is released, creating the concept of the blockbuster movie

19.event: Henry II becomes King of England at Westminster Abbey
General Washington crosses the Delaware River to attack British forces
A Russian plane crashes into the Black Sea, killing 92 including the Alexandrov Ensemble

20.event: Richard I of England is captured and held for ransom by Duke Leopold of Austria
The last survivors of Napoleon's Grande Armée stagger out of Russia
Jacqueline Kennedy marries Greek shipping magnate Aristotle Onassis

21.event: The Mayflower Pilgrims land at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts
The Bahá'í Faith is founded by the Báb in Shiraz, Persia
Lockerbie disaster: Pan Am Flight 103 explodes over Scotland, killing 270

22.event: Pope Honorius III approves the rule of the Dominican Order
Ludwig van Beethoven conducts the first performance of his 5th and 6th Symphonies
SpaceX launches its first spacecraft, the Dragon capsule

23.event: Childeric II, King of the Franks, is murdered
Clement Clarke Moore publishes 'A Visit from St. Nicholas' ('Twas the Night Before Christmas')
Apollo 17 returns to Earth — the last human Moon mission to date

24.event: Vasco da Gama, Portuguese explorer who found the sea route to India, dies in Cochin
'Silent Night' is performed for the first time in Oberndorf bei Salzburg, Austria
Apollo 8 astronauts become the first humans to orbit the Moon

25.event: Charlemagne is crowned Holy Roman Emperor by Pope Leo III at St. Peter's Basilica
William the Conqueror is crowned King of England at Westminster Abbey
Mikhail Gorbachev resigns as leader of the Soviet Union, which dissolves the next day

26.event: George Washington's forces defeat the Hessians at the Battle of Trenton
Marie and Pierre Curie announce the discovery of radium
A massive Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami kills over 230,000 people in 14 countries

27.event: The Hagia Sophia basilica in Constantinople is completed
Johannes Kepler, astronomer who described planetary motion, is born
General Patton's Third Army liberates the Buchenwald concentration camp

28.event: Boniface I is elected Pope
Wilhelm Röntgen takes the first X-ray image — of his wife's hand
Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn publishes 'The Gulag Archipelago' in Paris

29.event: Emperor Marcus Aurelius begins his philosophical work 'Meditations'
Archbishop Thomas Becket is murdered in Canterbury Cathedral
The first Gulf War ends with a cease-fire

30.event: Emperor Caligula's sister Agrippina the Younger gives birth to the future Emperor Nero
The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) is officially established
Saddam Hussein is executed by hanging in Baghdad, Iraq

31.event: Roman Emperor Commodus is assassinated by a conspiracy of the Praetorian Guard
Arthur Guinness signs a 9,000-year lease on the St. James's Gate Brewery in Dublin
The United States officially transfers control of the Panama Canal to Panama
